inline_comments.message: The patches look really trivial, wouldn't it be more elegant to rebase them on supervisor version that get's installed by default instead of pinning it's version for good? At some point the 4.1.0 version will get obsoleted and build of this dockerfile will fail for good.

I tried to update the patch to the newest v4.2.0 but I couldn't find a way to preserve the patch's main purpose: to make the supervisor messages distinguisable from other logging messages.
I also checked that all supervisor versions since 2009 are still available at PyPi, so I think sticking to v4.1.0 (released 19-Oct-19) won't be an issue for the next 5y, at least.
